content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to use explicit type casting to convert a precise int value into a char. (Use ASCII table for reference)

**When executed, the code will show:**

```default
Original int value: 120
Converted char value: x
```

---

**Relevant ASCII Table (Integer to Character)**

| Uppercase | ASCII | Lowercase | ASCII |
| --------- | ----- | --------- | ----- |
| A         | 65    | a         | 97    |
| B         | 66    | b         | 98    |
| C         | 67    | c         | 99    |
| D         | 68    | d         | 100   |
| E         | 69    | e         | 101   |
| F         | 70    | f         | 102   |
| G         | 71    | g         | 103   |
| H         | 72    | h         | 104   |
| I         | 73    | i         | 105   |
| J         | 74    | j         | 106   |
| K         | 75    | k         | 107   |
| L         | 76    | l         | 108   |
| M         | 77    | m         | 109   |
| N         | 78    | n         | 110   |
| O         | 79    | o         | 111   |
| P         | 80    | p         | 112   |
| Q         | 81    | q         | 113   |
| R         | 82    | r         | 114   |
| S         | 83    | s         | 115   |
| T         | 84    | t         | 116   |
| U         | 85    | u         | 117   |
| V         | 86    | v         | 118   |
| W         | 87    | w         | 119   |
| X         | 88    | x         | 120   |
| Y         | 89    | y         | 121   |
| Z         | 90    | z         | 122   |
